header_summary:
Junhyuk Lee's paper examines the economic shifts in Bitcoin mining behavior as the system transitions from block rewards to a fee-only model, highlighting a threshold where deviation from honest mining becomes profitable. Proposed solutions include base fees and adaptive block sizing to stabilize incentives and maintain network integrity, especially as small fee differentials could promote dishonest practices [arxiv](https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.05503). Further discussions and feedback on these mechanisms are available at [Research on Bitcoin Post-Block Rewards](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/research-bitcoin-after-block-rewards-preventing-miners-deviation-when-the-bitcoin-rewards-is-zero/2626) and [Addressing Diminishing Block Subsidy](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/addressing-the-diminishing-block-subsidy/2640).

The release of Bitcoin Core 31.1 introduces several enhancements including bug fixes, privacy improvements, and system stability across various operating systems, with comprehensive details available on [Bitcoin Core's official website](https://bitcoincore.org/bin/bitcoin-core-31.1/). Users are encouraged to engage with the development community through problem reporting and discussion platforms like [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/issues).

Emerging discussions in blockchain transaction management suggest a shift from traditional methods like mempool monitoring to more innovative approaches such as relay-safe expiry to combat specific types of network attacks. These evolving strategies are part of broader efforts to refine blockchain functionalities, with further insights offered in a detailed post by @ajtowns available [here](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/input-triggered-transaction-expiry/2667/3?u=josh).

The adaptation of blockchain to support post-quantum cryptography involves complex changes like creating an extension block for post-quantum signatures and exploring new transaction identifiers. While these adjustments offer a path forward for integrating advanced cryptographic measures, they also raise significant challenges in network compatibility and operational efficiency, elaborately discussed at [Delving Bitcoin](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/segwit-commitment-to-post-quantum-witness-data/2702).

recent_posts.title: [Research] Bitcoin After Block Rewards: preventing miner deviation as the block rewards approaches zero
recent_posts.summary: - The paper studies potential deviations in Bitcoin mining behavior when block rewards end.  - It identifies a threshold at which it becomes rational for miners to deviate.  - Strategies like base fees and adaptive block sizing are proposed to stabilize mining incentives.

recent_posts.title: Bitcoin Core v31.1 Released
recent_posts.summary:
- Bitcoin Core 31.1 brings bug fixes, performance enhancements, and updated translations.
- For upgrades, users must shut down their current version before starting the new installation.
- The update supports operating systems like Linux Kernel 3.17, macOS 14, and Windows 10 or newer.

recent_posts.title: Expiring HTLCs without free relay
recent_posts.summary: - The email discusses blockchain transaction management and potential improvements.  - It proposes relay-safe expiry to address time-sensitive replacement cycling attacks.  - The sender plans continued research and posting on evolving blockchain technologies.

recent_posts.title: Segwit commitment to post-quantum witness data?
recent_posts.summary:
- Enhancing blockchain technology introduces innovative adjustments for post-quantum cryptography.
- Proposed changes like creating a new transaction identifier add complexities to the network.
- Alternative solutions could integrate post-quantum data without major system overhauls.

recent_posts.title: Fountain Codes: a way to reduce blockchain storage costs
recent_posts.summary:
- Low-density parity check codes increase computational overheads without addressing main data issues.
- Peer limits impede scalability and miner sharing, thus affecting data handling efficiency.
- Major unresolved issues in system performance and scalability remain a significant challenge.

active_posts.title: DahLIAS: Discrete Logarithm-Based Interactive Aggregate Signatures
active_posts.summary:
- DahLIAS introduces a constant-size signature scheme for CISA, enhancing cryptographic protocols.
- The scheme supports secure, efficient frameworks and advances previous speculative constructions.
- DahLIAS features a two-round signing process and speed in verification, promising for future CISA.

active_posts.title: Giving teeth to expected EC disabling: P2XX(-T)(-ML)
active_posts.summary:
- Bitcoin community explores security upgrades for output types to anticipate quantum threats.
- Two disabling mechanisms, Tripwire and Miner Lockdown, have been proposed to fortify security.
- These integrations aim to ensure reliable activation of protective measures against cryptographic vulnerabilities.

active_posts.title: BIP draft: Full-Aggregation of BIP 340 Signatures
active_posts.summary: - A new Bitcoin Improvement Proposal focuses on efficient signature aggregation.  - Jonas Nick, Tim Ruffing, and Yannick Seurin developed the proposal for CISA.  - Stakeholder feedback is sought to refine this Bitcoin protocol integration.

active_posts.title: [BIP Draft] Segregated Data: a prunable, script-isolated block region for data carriage
active_posts.summary:
- SegData proposes a soft-fork for segregated data storage in Bitcoin blocks.
- It introduces modifications like exclusive Merkle roots and prunable, non-transactional data entries.
- Adopters could be data indexers and timestamping services, excluding script-reliant data types.

active_posts.title: Public key recovery for EC leaves in P2MR (BIP-360)
active_posts.summary:
- The proposal aims to optimize BIP-360 by recovering EC public keys from signatures.
- This recovery reduces witness size and aligns with Pay-to-Witness-Public-Key-Hash efficiency.
- It enhances blockchain efficiency and quantum resistance without script modification needs.

active_posts.title: State of the transaction privacy work in Bitcoin
active_posts.summary: - Technologies like Confidential Transactions aim to enhance Bitcoin's privacy via sidechains.  - Cross-Input Signature Aggregation could reduce costs, without inherent privacy improvements.  - Bitcoin privacy strategies now prioritize regulatory compatibility over foundational changes.

today_in_history_posts.title: Proposal: extend bip70 with OpenAlias
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- A proposal recommends extending BIP70 with domain alias verification via DNSSEC.
- The proposal introduces two methods: modified chain validation and DNSSEC with OpenAlias.
- Implementation has occurred in Electrum, available in version 2.4, prompting a new BIP number.

today_in_history_posts.title: Lightning modifications draft paper
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Rusty Russell released a draft for deploying Lightning Network nodes using ln-node software.
- The document, posted on ozlabs.org, is preliminary and seeks community feedback.
- This guide is intended to enhance Bitcoin transaction speeds by simplifying LN node setup.

today_in_history_posts.title: Deflationary money is a Good Thing
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Investment strategies split into passive and active, each involving varying levels of management.
- Passive investments suffer from alignment issues, potentially causing asset underutilization and societal impacts.
- Proposing central banks stabilize economies using inflationary and deflationary currencies, like Bitcoin.
